= Chapter 6: Quality, Reliability, and Operations Runbook

== 6.1 Reliability posture

Kosha's operations model is designed around safe iteration on financial workflows:

. Every release is gated by deterministic script checks.
. Runtime failures are captured with context and routed into structured bug reporting.
. Critical financial writes emit audit events and retry non-blocking telemetry.
. Realtime freshness is treated as a reliability enhancer with fallback behavior.

Core runtime and release anchors:

. Release candidate orchestrator in link:{repo-blob-url}/scripts/ops/release_candidate_check.mjs#L1[scripts/ops/release_candidate_check.mjs]
. Deploy readiness probe in link:{repo-blob-url}/scripts/ops/check_deploy_readiness.mjs#L1[scripts/ops/check_deploy_readiness.mjs]
. Runtime monitor startup in link:{repo-blob-url}/src/main.jsx#L8[src/main.jsx]
. Global crash capture boundary in link:{repo-blob-url}/src/components/errors/GlobalErrorBoundary.jsx#L8[src/components/errors/GlobalErrorBoundary.jsx]
. Financial audit logger in link:{repo-blob-url}/src/lib/auditLog.js#L1[src/lib/auditLog.js]

'''

== 6.2 Build and release pipeline

== 6.2.1 NPM entrypoints

The operational command surface is centralized in link:{repo-blob-url}/package.json#L6[package.json]:

. Build and preview: link:{repo-blob-url}/package.json#L8[package.json]
. Release candidate check: link:{repo-blob-url}/package.json#L11[package.json]
. Deploy readiness: link:{repo-blob-url}/package.json#L12[package.json]
. Domain tests (mutation, reconciliation, join-flow, realtime, production assets): link:{repo-blob-url}/package.json#L13[package.json]

This creates a single executable release grammar for local and CI usage.

== 6.2.2 Release candidate orchestration

The release orchestrator executes a strict sequence in link:{repo-blob-url}/scripts/ops/release_candidate_check.mjs#L3[scripts/ops/release_candidate_check.mjs]:

. Build
. Mutation path checks
. Reconciliation checks
. Deploy readiness checks
. Join and realtime checks
. Stress checks

Operational behavior:

. Each step streams output and records duration in link:{repo-blob-url}/scripts/ops/release_candidate_check.mjs#L23[scripts/ops/release_candidate_check.mjs].
. Flaky network-sensitive checks can retry (for example join-flow and liabilities-realtime) in link:{repo-blob-url}/scripts/ops/release_candidate_check.mjs#L14[scripts/ops/release_candidate_check.mjs].
. Execution halts at first failure and emits a summary table in link:{repo-blob-url}/scripts/ops/release_candidate_check.mjs#L89[scripts/ops/release_candidate_check.mjs].

== 6.2.3 Deploy readiness guard

Deploy readiness is a schema and RPC contract probe in link:{repo-blob-url}/scripts/ops/check_deploy_readiness.mjs#L1[scripts/ops/check_deploy_readiness.mjs].

It verifies:

. Table accessibility and required columns for transactions/liabilities in link:{repo-blob-url}/scripts/ops/check_deploy_readiness.mjs#L60[scripts/ops/check_deploy_readiness.mjs].
. Financial events and reconciliation review schema availability in link:{repo-blob-url}/scripts/ops/check_deploy_readiness.mjs#L89[scripts/ops/check_deploy_readiness.mjs].
. mark_liability_paid and generate_recurring_transactions RPC availability in link:{repo-blob-url}/scripts/ops/check_deploy_readiness.mjs#L105[scripts/ops/check_deploy_readiness.mjs].

Critical feature:

. Missing RPC/table conditions are surfaced with migration-specific guidance, reducing ambiguous deployment failures.

'''

== 6.3 Environment and credential model

== 6.3.1 Script env loading

All Node-based scripts load local env files via link:{repo-blob-url}/scripts/load_env.mjs#L1[scripts/load_env.mjs]:

. `.env` then `.env.local`
. Existing process env values are not overridden
. Quoted values are normalized

This keeps script behavior reproducible while supporting local overrides.

== 6.3.2 Required operational env vars

Release and test scripts depend on:

. Supabase runtime vars (`VITE_SUPABASE_URL`, `VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY`) documented in link:{repo-blob-url}/README.md#L179[README.md].
. E2E account vars (`E2E_*`) documented in link:{repo-blob-url}/README.md#L186[README.md].
. Optional production integrity target (`APP_URL`) in link:{repo-blob-url}/scripts/tests/test_production_asset_integrity.mjs#L1[scripts/tests/test_production_asset_integrity.mjs].

== 6.3.3 Edge function secrets

Bug-notify function requires:

. `SUPABASE_URL`
. `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY`
. `BUG_REPORT_WEBHOOK_URL`

These are consumed in link:{repo-blob-url}/supabase/functions/bug-report-notify/index.ts#L24[supabase/functions/bug-report-notify/index.ts].

'''

== 6.4 Verification suite architecture

Kosha's test suite is scenario-first rather than unit-only. Each script validates a production risk lane.

Script index is documented in link:{repo-blob-url}/scripts/README.md#L1[scripts/README.md].

== 6.4.1 Contract and safety checks

. Mutation rollback contract static verification in link:{repo-blob-url}/scripts/tests/test_mutation_rollback_contract.mjs#L1[scripts/tests/test_mutation_rollback_contract.mjs].
. Reconciliation schema live check in link:{repo-blob-url}/scripts/tests/test_reconciliation_schema_live.mjs#L1[scripts/tests/test_reconciliation_schema_live.mjs].
. Deploy readiness schema/RPC probes in link:{repo-blob-url}/scripts/ops/check_deploy_readiness.mjs#L58[scripts/ops/check_deploy_readiness.mjs].

Why this matters:

. These checks fail fast when migrations are missing or rollback guarantees regress.

== 6.4.2 Behavior and workflow checks

. Join invite lifecycle in link:{repo-blob-url}/scripts/tests/test_join_flow.mjs#L1[scripts/tests/test_join_flow.mjs].
. Reconciliation persistence and alias reset behavior in link:{repo-blob-url}/scripts/tests/test_reconciliation_flow.mjs#L1[scripts/tests/test_reconciliation_flow.mjs].
. Mutation stress lifecycle for transactions and liabilities in link:{repo-blob-url}/scripts/tests/test_mutation_stress.mjs#L1[scripts/tests/test_mutation_stress.mjs].

Why this matters:

. These tests reflect user-facing workflows, not isolated helper behavior.

== 6.4.3 Realtime delivery checks

Realtime reliability is explicitly validated in link:{repo-blob-url}/scripts/tests/test_liabilities_realtime.mjs#L1[scripts/tests/test_liabilities_realtime.mjs]:

. Dual-session sign-in (actor/listener)
. Channel subscription handshake
. Timed insert event delivery
. Retries across transient channel failures

WebSocket fallback behavior:

. For Node environments without native WebSocket, `ws` polyfill import is attempted in link:{repo-blob-url}/scripts/tests/test_liabilities_realtime.mjs#L20[scripts/tests/test_liabilities_realtime.mjs].

== 6.4.4 Production asset integrity checks

Deployed artifact sanity is tested by link:{repo-blob-url}/scripts/tests/test_production_asset_integrity.mjs#L1[scripts/tests/test_production_asset_integrity.mjs]:

. Fetches deployed HTML
. Extracts hashed JS/CSS asset URLs
. Verifies asset fetch and content-type correctness
. Emits manual post-deploy service-worker hygiene step

This complements build success by validating deployment output, not only local dist generation.

'''

== 6.5 Runtime resilience architecture

== 6.5.1 Startup monitoring

Runtime monitor starts before React render in link:{repo-blob-url}/src/main.jsx#L8[src/main.jsx].

It captures:

. Window script errors
. Unhandled promise rejections
. Recent route trail and event buffers

Implementation in link:{repo-blob-url}/src/lib/runtimeMonitor.js#L1[src/lib/runtimeMonitor.js] stores bounded diagnostics in sessionStorage.

== 6.5.2 Global crash handling

Rendering crashes are trapped by GlobalErrorBoundary in link:{repo-blob-url}/src/components/errors/GlobalErrorBoundary.jsx#L8[src/components/errors/GlobalErrorBoundary.jsx].

Behavior:

. Displays recovery UI with reload/home/report options.
. Prepares structured runtime prefill payload for bug report flow in link:{repo-blob-url}/src/components/errors/GlobalErrorBoundary.jsx#L26[src/components/errors/GlobalErrorBoundary.jsx].
. Navigates directly to Report Bug with crash context.

== 6.5.3 Query performance tracing

Query timing trace utility exists in link:{repo-blob-url}/src/lib/queryTrace.js#L1[src/lib/queryTrace.js].

Key properties:

. Dev-only activation by localStorage flag.
. Zero overhead when disabled.
. Consistent timing logs for network/query bottleneck diagnosis.

== 6.5.4 Realtime fallback behavior

GlobalRealtimeSync in link:{repo-blob-url}/src/App.jsx#L535[src/App.jsx] provides resilient freshness behavior:

. Subscribes to table change events.
. Applies delayed family invalidation.
. Falls back to polling when socket becomes unavailable.
. Reconnects with progressive retry delays.

This prevents hard dependency on websocket continuity.

'''

== 6.6 Auditability and financial event logging

Financial mutation telemetry is implemented in link:{repo-blob-url}/src/lib/auditLog.js#L1[src/lib/auditLog.js].

=== Action taxonomy

Standardized action types include:

. Transaction add/update/delete
. Liability add/mark-paid/delete
. Loan add/payment/delete

Defined in link:{repo-blob-url}/src/lib/auditLog.js#L3[src/lib/auditLog.js].

=== Delivery semantics

. Logger retries failed inserts with bounded exponential-like delay in link:{repo-blob-url}/src/lib/auditLog.js#L16[src/lib/auditLog.js].
. Missing `financial_events` table is treated as safe no-op for partially migrated environments in link:{repo-blob-url}/src/lib/auditLog.js#L39[src/lib/auditLog.js].
. Final failures are warning-logged and do not block user mutations.

Operational implication:

. Business mutations stay available even when telemetry path degrades.
. Audit trail remains best-effort durable under transient backend errors.

'''

== 6.7 Bug intake and triage pipeline

Kosha has an end-to-end in-app bug pipeline from crash to webhook notification.

== 6.7.1 Client-side report creation

ReportBug page entry is in link:{repo-blob-url}/src/pages/ReportBug.jsx#L24[src/pages/ReportBug.jsx].

Capabilities:

. Structured title/description/steps/severity/tags input.
. Optional screenshot compression and upload in link:{repo-blob-url}/src/pages/ReportBug.jsx#L111[src/pages/ReportBug.jsx].
. Fingerprint and diagnostics packaging via utility helpers from link:{repo-blob-url}/src/lib/bugReportUtils.js#L1[src/lib/bugReportUtils.js].

Submission path:

. RPC `submit_bug_report` call in link:{repo-blob-url}/src/pages/ReportBug.jsx#L184[src/pages/ReportBug.jsx].
. Optional edge notification invoke in link:{repo-blob-url}/src/pages/ReportBug.jsx#L207[src/pages/ReportBug.jsx].

== 6.7.2 Bug utility helpers

Utility module link:{repo-blob-url}/src/lib/bugReportUtils.js#L1[src/lib/bugReportUtils.js] provides:

. Stable fingerprint generation for dedupe in link:{repo-blob-url}/src/lib/bugReportUtils.js#L5[src/lib/bugReportUtils.js].
. Tag normalization with strict character policy in link:{repo-blob-url}/src/lib/bugReportUtils.js#L22[src/lib/bugReportUtils.js].
. Client-side image compression for large screenshot payloads in link:{repo-blob-url}/src/lib/bugReportUtils.js#L41[src/lib/bugReportUtils.js].

== 6.7.3 Edge notification bridge

Edge function implementation in link:{repo-blob-url}/supabase/functions/bug-report-notify/index.ts#L1[supabase/functions/bug-report-notify/index.ts]:

. Loads bug report via service-role client.
. Generates signed screenshot URL when available.
. Posts to configured webhook (Discord-compatible or generic JSON).
. Marks report `notified_at` on success.

This separates user-report persistence from external notification reliability.

'''

== 6.8 Deployment and hosting behavior

== 6.8.1 SPA rewrite contract

Hosting rewrite behavior is defined in link:{repo-blob-url}/vercel.json#L1[vercel.json]:

. Filesystem handler first
. Fallback route rewrite to `index.html` for non-file paths

This is required for client-side routed pages like `/reconciliation` and `/report-bug`.

== 6.8.2 Build-time hardening

Vite configuration in link:{repo-blob-url}/vite.config.js#L1[vite.config.js] enforces:

. Console/debugger stripping via esbuild drop in link:{repo-blob-url}/vite.config.js#L35[vite.config.js].
. Deterministic vendor chunk splitting in link:{repo-blob-url}/vite.config.js#L5[vite.config.js].
. PWA runtime caching policy for Supabase REST and font assets in link:{repo-blob-url}/vite.config.js#L67[vite.config.js].

Operational meaning:

. Smaller, cache-friendly bundles.
. Offline-capable repeat visits.
. Controlled stale-while-revalidate data behavior aligned with query-layer freshness.

'''

== 6.9 Release and incident runbook

== 6.9.1 Pre-release checklist

. Ensure env vars are present and valid.
. Run full build and release candidate checks.
. Confirm deploy-readiness pass against target Supabase project.
. Validate production assets after deploy.

Commands:

. `npm run build`
. `npm run test:deploy-readiness`
. `npm run release:candidate-check`
. `npm run test:production-assets`

Entrypoint references:

. link:{repo-blob-url}/README.md#L285[README.md]
. link:{repo-blob-url}/scripts/ops/release_candidate_check.mjs#L1[scripts/ops/release_candidate_check.mjs]

== 6.9.2 If release-candidate check fails

. Stop promotion immediately.
. Identify first failed stage from release summary output.
. Re-run only failed check locally after fix.
. Re-run full release candidate suite before shipping.

The orchestrator intentionally fails-fast in link:{repo-blob-url}/scripts/ops/release_candidate_check.mjs#L75[scripts/ops/release_candidate_check.mjs], so first failure is usually the most actionable signal.

== 6.9.3 If realtime checks fail intermittently

. Re-run liabilities realtime script (it has internal retries).
. Verify websocket availability (`ws` fallback path for Node).
. Validate Supabase realtime configuration and network constraints.

Reference: link:{repo-blob-url}/scripts/tests/test_liabilities_realtime.mjs#L20[scripts/tests/test_liabilities_realtime.mjs].

== 6.9.4 If reconciliation schema checks fail

. Apply latest `supabase/schema.sql`.
. Re-run reconciliation schema live check.
. Re-run deploy readiness script.

References:

. link:{repo-blob-url}/scripts/tests/test_reconciliation_schema_live.mjs#L50[scripts/tests/test_reconciliation_schema_live.mjs]
. link:{repo-blob-url}/scripts/ops/check_deploy_readiness.mjs#L97[scripts/ops/check_deploy_readiness.mjs]

== 6.9.5 If runtime crash reports spike

. Inspect bug_reports entries and occurrence_count trends.
. Use captured route + runtime diagnostics payload from bug reports.
. Correlate with recent release changes and query timings (if trace enabled).
. Patch and verify via focused scenario scripts before broad release.

'''

== 6.10 Operational guardrails for contributors

. Never ship schema-dependent changes without running deploy-readiness checks.
. Keep mutation rollback guarantees intact when refactoring hook mutation wrappers.
. Preserve bug report fields and fingerprint behavior to avoid triage regressions.
. Treat release-candidate check as mandatory, not optional.
. Keep realtime and fallback paths functional; test both optimistic and eventual-consistency behavior.
. Do not bypass runtime error boundary or monitor wiring in app bootstrap.

Adhering to these guardrails keeps Kosha financially trustworthy under both normal and failure conditions.
